title: Heat flow in the southern Chile forearc controlled by large-scale tectonic processes
abstract: Between 33°S and 47°S, heat flow through the southern Chile forearc is estimated using the depth of the bottom-simulating reflector obtained from a comprehensive database of reflection seismic profiles. On the upper and middle continental slope, heat flow is 30–60 mW/m², whereas the actively deforming accretionary wedge on the lower slope shows heat flow reaching ~90 mW/m², indicating advecting pore fluids from deeper in the subduction zone. Large-scale tectonic features including the subducting Juan Fernández Ridge, oceanic fracture zones, and the Chile Ridge spreading center modulate the heat flow pattern.

location.description: Southern Chile forearc from 33°S to 47°S; heat flow from BSR depth on comprehensive seismic database

seismic_lines.name: RC2901-738
seismic_lines.notes: Southern sector 44°–45°S strike line, heat flow 80–100 mW/m²

seismic_lines.name: RC2901-745
seismic_lines.notes: Southern sector, Chile Triple Junction area; ODP Site 859

seismic_lines.name: RC2901-751
seismic_lines.notes: Southern sector, above subducted Chile Ridge; ODP Site 863

seismic_lines.name: RC2901-756
seismic_lines.notes: Southern sector strike line, heat flow ~200 mW/m²

seismic_lines.name: VG06-33
seismic_lines.notes: Northern sector, BSR uplift above blind thrust

seismic_lines.name: VG06-74
seismic_lines.notes: Northern sector, BSR uplift above normal fault half-graben

data.name: R/V Conrad RC2901 MCS Profiles — Chile Margin 32°–48°S
data.description: R/V Conrad cruise RC2901 (Jan–Feb 1988) MCS profiles, 3000 m streamer, 240 ch, 61.34 L airguns. Openly available at UTIG Seismic Data Center (acknowledged by authors).

data.name: R/V SONNE SO161 SPOC MCS Profiles — Chile Margin 28°–44°S
data.description: R/V SONNE SO161 (Jan–Feb 2001) SPOC cruise MCS profiles, 3000 m streamer, 132 ch, 51.2 L airguns. GEOMAR data.

data.name: R/V Vidal Gormaz VG02/VG06 MCS Profiles — Chile Margin 32°–40°S
data.description: R/V Vidal Gormaz cruises VG02 (2002, FONDEF D00I11004) and VG06 (Feb 2006). VG06: 600 m streamer, 96 ch, 160 cui (4×SG-1 sleeve guns). Time-migrated data provided by FONDEF programme contributors.

_data_note: RC2901 data openly available at http://www.ig.utexas.edu/sdc/ (UTIG SDC). SO161 at GEOMAR/PANGAEA. Vidal Gormaz data via FONDEF programme. SO-101 CONDOR data from GEOMAR.

processing.summary: BSR depths from a comprehensive database of MCS reflection profiles along the southern Chile forearc (33°–47°S) are mapped and converted to heat flow values using gas hydrate stability theory. The resulting heat flow map (30–90 mW/m²) reveals latitudinal patterns modulated by large-scale tectonic features: Juan Fernández Ridge subduction at ~33°S, oceanic fracture zones, and the Chile Ridge spreading center at ~46°S.
workflow: 1. MCS database compilation from five cruises (RC2901, SO-101, SO161, VG02, VG06)
2. BSR identification by reversed polarity relative to seafloor reflection
3. TWT to depth conversion using cruise-specific velocity–depth functions (Kaul et al. 2000) or ODP drillhole data
4. Seafloor temperature from World Ocean Data Base CTD profiles
5. Thermal conductivity k from ODP Leg 141 cores (k=1.25 W/mK, southern sector) and ODP Leg 202 cores (k=0.85 W/mK, central/northern sector)
6. Heat flow q = (Tz−T0) / integral(k dz) using Dickens & Quinby-Hunt (1994) BSR temperature–pressure function
7. BSR depth and heat flow spatial mapping (colour-coded maps 33°–47°S)
8. Calibration with near-surface probe and borehole temperature data
9. Tectonic interpretation: Juan Fernández Ridge, fracture zones, Chile Ridge
processing.notes: Large-scale heat flow synthesis paper using five cruise datasets; no new acquisition

analysis_notes: PDF confirmed. Corrections applied: vessel list expanded to 5 cruises (RC2901, SO-101 CONDOR, SO161, VG02, VG06); year_acquired now a list [1988,1995,2001,2002,2006]; acquisition_details added per cruise; seismic_lines updated to 9 named profiles (SO161-34, RC2901-727/-731/-738/-745/-751/-756, VG06-33/-74); data entries split into 3 (RC2901 open at UTIG SDC http://www.ig.utexas.edu/sdc/, SO161 GEOMAR/PANGAEA, VG02/VG06 FONDEF); software confirmed GMT; workflow expanded to 9 steps.
